build.details: Build and lint pass with 0 errors. 10 pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings in unrelated blocks (Accordion, Archive, ContentWithMedia, EventHero, FullWidthMedia, PostHero, Tabs) — not introduced by this change. Sync: all checks pass. Root wrapper mismatch warning (InspectorControls vs section) is a known false-positive for editor components using sidebar controls.

brTagFix.details:
Verified via raw block content and rendered HTML that
tags in hoursContent and contactContent render as actual HTML line breaks, not escaped <br> strings.

longContentInstance.note:
\u003cbr\u003e is JSON-escaped
— stored correctly in block attributes

fullContentInstance.note: Plain newlines in simple content — wp_kses_post passes these through as text nodes

renderedHTML.hoursColumn:
Monday through Friday:
8:30am – 4:30pm
Saturday:
10:00am – 2:00pm
Sunday:
Closed
Holiday Hours:
Please check our website for holiday-specific hours and closures
renderedHTML.contactColumn:
Main Office: 416.675.5051
Student Services: 416.675.5052
email@verylongemailaddress.institution.com
support@verylongemailaddress.institution.com
renderedHTML.verdict:
Actual
tags present in rendered output — not escaped. wp_kses_post correctly allows
and renders it as a real HTML line break.

brTagFix.renderMechanism:
PHP template uses wp_kses_post() for hoursContent and contactContent. wp_kses_post() allows
tags and outputs them unescaped, so the stored \u003cbr\u003e (JSON-decoded to
) renders as a real HTML line break in the browser.

addressTwoPerRow.details: DOM audit confirms sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-48 class present on address items in all 5 instances that have address data. Full Content instance (index 0): 2 items — both appear in a single row at 1024px+. Long Content instance (index 2): 3 items — 2 on first row, 1 wraps to second row. Correct flex-wrap behavior. The fluid layout from the previous fix (sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-48 replacing sm:w-[calc(50%-1rem)]) remains intact.

tsxPhpSync.details: taktician_validate_all sync check passed with 0 errors. All attribute extractions use auto-extracted variables. PHP docblock annotations present. Known false-positive on root wrapper mismatch (InspectorControls fragment pattern).

breakpoints.observation:
Mobile: all sections stack vertically. Long Content instance clearly shows Hours column with multi-line
-separated content — 'Monday through Friday:' on its own line followed by times, Saturday, Sunday, Holiday Hours. Each
creates a visible line break. Address items are in single-column stacked layout. Contact column similarly shows phone and email on separate lines. No overflow or escaped tag strings visible anywhere.

Tablet: right group (Hours/Contact/Follow Us) transitions to flex-row.
line breaks remain visible in Hours and Contact columns at this width — content is readable and not cramped. Address items start to flex-wrap side by side.

Desktop mid: Full Content instance shows 2 address items cleanly side by side. Long Content instance shows 3 addresses with 2+1 wrap. Right group with Hours/Contact/Follow Us distributes evenly.
content in Hours and Contact columns renders correctly with line breaks creating clear visual separation between entries.

Desktop wide: Same as 1024 observations — 2 address items per row confirmed in Full Content instance. Long Content instance 2+1 wrap. lg:flex-[0_1_auto] right group does not over-expand.
-based line breaks in Hours and Contact content are crisp and legible. No regressions visible across all 6 footer instances.

html.reason: HTML changed from previous snapshot — full QA run performed

passedChecks: Build and lint pass with 0 errors
tags in hoursContent render as real HTML line breaks (not escaped <br>) via wp_kses_post()
tags in contactContent render as real HTML line breaks
Address items show 2 per row at 1024px and 1440px via sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-48
Long Content instance (3 addresses) wraps 2+1 correctly

Mobile 375px:
line breaks create visible line separation in Hours and Contact columns
Responsive layout intact across all 6 footer instances at all 4 breakpoints
12 screenshots captured across 3 browsers x 4 widths
